Defines structures and functions for configuring and managing a time selection window interface.

# Purpose
The code defines a C header file for managing a time selection window interface. It provides structures and functions to initialize, configure, and deinitialize a time selection window, which is part of a user interface. The main components include the `TimeSelectionWindowData` structure, which holds various UI elements such as `Window`, `SelectionLayer`, `TextLayer`, and `StatusBarLayer`, as well as time-related data and callback functions. The `TimeSelectionWindowConfig` structure allows configuration of the window's label, color, range settings, and callback functions.

The file includes function declarations for [`time_selection_window_set_to_current_time`](<#time_selection_window_set_to_current_time>), [`time_selection_window_configure`](<#time_selection_window_configure>), [`time_selection_window_init`](<#time_selection_window_init>), and [`time_selection_window_deinit`](<#time_selection_window_deinit>). These functions manage the lifecycle and behavior of the time selection window. The header file also defines a callback type, `TimeSelectionCompleteCallback`, which is used to notify when a time selection is complete. The code is intended to be used as part of a larger application, providing a specific interface for time selection within a graphical user interface.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `date_time_selection_window_private.h`
- `applib/ui/selection_layer.h`
- `applib/ui/status_bar_layer.h`
- `applib/ui/text_layer.h`
- `applib/ui/window.h`
- `services/common/clock.h`
- `time.h`


# Data Structures

---
### TimeSelectionWindowData
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``window``: Holds the main window for the time selection interface.
    - ``selection_layer``: Manages the selection layer for user interaction.
    - ``label_text_layer``: Displays the label text in the window.
    - ``range_subtitle_text_layer``: Displays the subtitle text for the range.
    - ``range_text_layer``: Displays the range text in the window.
    - ``status_layer``: Displays the status bar layer.
    - ``time_data``: Stores the time data for the selection.
    - ``complete_callback``: Function pointer for the callback when selection is complete.
    - ``callback_context``: Context data for the callback function.
    - ``range_text``: Pointer to the range text string.
    - ``range_duration_m``: Stores the duration of the range in minutes.
    - ``range_buf``: Buffer for storing time strings.
    - ``range_subtitle_buf``: Buffer for storing the range label.
    - ``cell_buf``: Buffer for storing cell data.
- **Description**: Manages the data and interface elements necessary for a time selection window, including window management, text display layers, and callback functionality for when a time selection is complete. It includes buffers for storing time and range information, as well as a callback mechanism to handle completion events.


---
### TimeSelectionWindowConfig
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``label``: A pointer to a constant character string that represents the label for the time selection window.
    - ``color``: A `GColor` value that specifies the color associated with the time selection window.
    - ``range``: A nested structure that contains fields for updating, text, duration in minutes, and enabling the range.
    - ``callback``: A nested structure that contains fields for updating, a completion callback, and a context pointer.
- **Description**: Defines the configuration for a time selection window, including a label, color, range settings, and callback settings. The `range` structure allows configuration of the time range with options to update, specify text, set duration, and enable or disable the range. The `callback` structure provides options to update, specify a completion callback function, and pass a context pointer.

### time\_selection\_window\_set\_to\_current\_time<!-- {{#callable_declaration:time_selection_window_set_to_current_time}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/time_selection_window.h#L73>)

Sets the time selection window to the current system time.
- **Description**: Use this function to update the time data in a `TimeSelectionWindowData` structure to reflect the current system time. This function is useful when you need to synchronize the time selection window with the current time. Ensure that the `time_selection_window` parameter is a valid pointer to a `TimeSelectionWindowData` structure before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `time_selection_window`: A pointer to a `TimeSelectionWindowData` structure. Must not be null. The function updates the `hour` and `minute` fields of the `time_data` member with the current system time.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`time_selection_window_set_to_current_time`](<time_selection_window.c.md#time_selection_window_set_to_current_time>)  (Implementation)

### time\_selection\_window\_configure<!-- {{#callable_declaration:time_selection_window_configure}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/time_selection_window.h#L75>)

Configures a time selection window with specified settings.
- **Description**: Use this function to set up a time selection window with specific configuration options, such as label text, background color, range settings, and completion callback. This function must be called after the time selection window is initialized. It updates the window's appearance and behavior based on the provided configuration. Ensure that the configuration structure is correctly populated before calling this function to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**:
    - `time_selection_window`: A pointer to a `TimeSelectionWindowData` structure that represents the time selection window to configure. Must not be null.
    - `config`: A pointer to a `TimeSelectionWindowConfig` structure containing the desired configuration settings. Must not be null. The structure should have valid values for label, color, range, and callback fields as needed.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`time_selection_window_configure`](<time_selection_window.c.md#time_selection_window_configure>)  (Implementation)

### time\_selection\_window\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:time_selection_window_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/time_selection_window.h#L78>)

Initializes a time selection window with the specified configuration.
- **Description**: Use this function to set up a time selection window with the given configuration. It prepares the window and its components, such as selection layers and text layers, for user interaction. This function must be called before using the time selection window to ensure all elements are properly initialized. The function does not handle invalid input, so ensure that the provided pointers are valid and the configuration is correctly set up before calling.
- **Inputs**:
    - `time_selection_window`: A pointer to a `TimeSelectionWindowData` structure that will be initialized. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
    - `config`: A pointer to a `TimeSelectionWindowConfig` structure containing the configuration for the window. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`time_selection_window_init`](<time_selection_window.c.md#time_selection_window_init>)  (Implementation)

### time\_selection\_window\_deinit<!-- {{#callable_declaration:time_selection_window_deinit}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/time_selection_window.h#L81>)

Deinitializes a time selection window.
- **Description**: Use this function to deinitialize a `TimeSelectionWindowData` object when it is no longer needed. This function releases resources associated with the status and selection layers within the time selection window. Ensure that the `time_selection_window` parameter is not null before calling this function to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**:
    - `time_selection_window`: A pointer to a `TimeSelectionWindowData` object. Must not be null. The function does nothing if this parameter is null.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`time_selection_window_deinit`](<time_selection_window.c.md#time_selection_window_deinit>)  (Implementation)
